content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

BAGATELOS & FADEM

BARRY FADEM
PETER A. BAGATELOS
WES VAN WINKLE

THE INTERNATIONAL BUILDING
601 CALIFORNIA STREET
SUITE 1801
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108

TELEPHONE
(415) 982-7100
FAX
(415) 982-1085

July 21, 1988


Ms. Carla Wardlow
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: County Major Donor Committees

Dear Carla:

     This will confirm our telephone conversation on July 21, 1988. I asked you whether a major donor whose primary purpose is to support a San Francisco ballot measure would be a city or a county major donor committee.  San Francisco is both a City and a County.

     After conferring with Jeanne Pritchard and Kathy Donovan, you advised me that such an entity should be treated as a county major donor committee.

     Thank you very much for this advice and assistance, we will advise our client and accordingly act in reliance on this information.

                                             Very truly yours,

                                             [signature]
                                             Peter A. Bagatelos


PAB/yjw

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 27, 1988


Peter A. Bagatelos
Bagatelos & Fadem
601 California Street, Suite 1801
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: 88-295

Dear Mr. Bagatelos:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on July 22, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

JP:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a major donor committee whose primary purpose is to support a San Francisco ballot measure should be classified as a city or county major donor committee under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The major donor committee in question should be treated as a county major donor committee, as advised by FPPC staff after consultation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two letters: (1) attorney's letter to FPPC staff documenting informal telephone advice received, and (2) FPPC's acknowledgment/receipt letter. The formal advice conclusion is documented in the attorney's letter rather than in a separate FPPC advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in typical format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a major donor committee whose primary purpose is to support a San Francisco ballot measure should be classified as a city or county major donor committee under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The major donor committee in question should be treated as a county major donor committee, as advised by FPPC staff after consultation.

embedding.summary: This document contains correspondence regarding an informal advice request about the proper classification of a major donor committee for a San Francisco ballot measure. The FPPC's initial response letter (July 27, 1988) acknowledges receipt of the request and outlines the commission's timeline for responses, while an attached letter from the attorney confirms prior telephone advice that such an entity should be treated as a county major donor committee.
